privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy explains how personal data is processed in the internal FIFA World Cup 2026 prediction game. The application is intended for employees and teams within the organisation. If possible, please register with a personal email address (not a work/company address).

controller.title: 1. Data controller
controller.text: The organisation is responsible for processing personal data in this application. If you have privacy-related questions, please contact the address provided below.

dataCollected.title: 2. Data we process
items: Account data: first name, last name, email address and password (stored as a hash)
Team assignment: link to an internal team (e.g. department or project group)
Profile data: profile picture, language preference, favourite national team and top scorer pick
Game data: predictions, bonus answers, points, leaderboard rank and statistics
Usage data: notifications, optional AI coach requests and technical logs
Communication data: email verification and password reset tokens
Visibility: name, team, profile picture, predictions and favourites may be visible to other registered participants

purpose.title: 3. Purposes of processing
items: Providing and operating the prediction game
Managing user accounts and team rankings
Calculating points, leaderboards and statistics
Sending email reminders and in-app notifications
Security, error analysis and abuse prevention
Optional AI features for match analysis and assistance

teamData.title: 4. Team and colleague data
teamData.text: Because the game is used on a team basis, your data is linked with other participants in your organisation for team rankings and leaderboards. This includes in particular your name, assigned team, predictions, points and – if provided – your World Cup favourites. Data is not published outside the organisation.

legalBasis.title: 5. Legal bases
items: Contract performance or pre-contractual steps (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) for account and game features
Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) in operation, security and internal team rankings
Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) where you use optional features or email reminders
Legal obligations (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR) where statutory retention duties apply

sharing.title: 6. Sharing and processors
items: Within the organisation: visibility for registered participants according to game rules
IT operations: hosting and technical operation of the application
Email service: delivery of verification, reset and reminder emails
External sports data APIs: retrieval of schedules, results and team data (your account data is not shared)
Optional AI features: if enabled, inputs and context data are transferred to an external AI/LLM provider (e.g. OpenAI) to generate AI responses
Error/performance monitoring: if configured, technical error data may be sent to a monitoring service (e.g. Sentry)
No sale of personal data to third parties

retention.title: 7. Retention
retention.text: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to operate the prediction game or as required by law. You can delete your account at any time in your profile. This permanently removes your user account and related game data. You can remove optional profile data (e.g. profile picture) at any time in your profile. Logs of optional AI interactions, if enabled, are deleted after 90 days by default and removed upon account deletion.

rights.title: 8. Your rights
items: Access to the personal data we process about you
Rectification of inaccurate data in your profile
Deletion of your account and related data
Restriction of processing where legally applicable
Objection to processing based on legitimate interests
Complaint to a competent data protection supervisory authority

security.title: 9. Data security
security.text: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data against loss, misuse and unauthorised access. These include hashed password storage, role-based access controls and secure server connections.

contact.title: 10. Contact
contact.text: For questions about this privacy policy or to exercise your rights, please contact your administrator or the designated contact person for the prediction game in your organisation.

help.title: Release Train Engineer
help.subtitle: How the World Cup 2026 pool works — fair, transparent, and without manual workarounds.

winner.title: Who wins?
winner.text: The player with the most points on the leaderboard wins. There is also a team ranking based on each department's average points per member (e.g. IT, Finance).

scoring.title: Points per match
scoring.intro: For each match you predict the score. After full time, only the best category counts — not multiple at once:

deadline.title: When can I predict?
deadline.text: Predictions are allowed until kick-off. After that, matches are locked — including live, finished, or admin-locked matches.

participation.title: Do I have to predict every match?
participation.intro: The system contains 104 World Cup matches (72 group games + 32 knockout ties). There is no technical or legal requirement to complete every match — you can predict only selected games. Missing predictions score 0 points and lower your completion rate on the leaderboard.
participation.rteRule: RTE rule for this pool: every participant predicts all matches themselves. Splitting work among colleagues (e.g. “you take Group A, I take Group B”) is not allowed.
participation.tableNote: The highlighted row is the model we use. The other variants do not apply in this pool.

profile.title: Profile: favorite team & top scorer
profile.text: Set your favorite national team and top scorer pick in your profile. Your favorite team is used for the bonus question “How far will your team get?” and is visible to all participants.

bonus.title: Bonus questions
bonus.text: In addition to matches, there are bonus questions (e.g. winner, top scorer). They award extra points that count towards your total score.

leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
leaderboard.text: The leaderboard shows your rank, match points, bonus points, and total. You can filter by total, match, or bonus points.

teams.title: Team ranking
teams.text: Each player belongs to a team. The team ranking uses average points per member — the largest department does not win automatically.

ai.title: Ask the AI coach
ai.text: The AI coach can help with strategy, missing tips, and points. It does not replace official rules — points are always calculated by the system.

ai.askPlaceholder: Ask your AI coach a question…
ai.leaderboardSummaryRateLimit: Daily AI commentary limit reached. Available again tomorrow.

sync.title: Synchronization
sync.footballDataHint: Primary football API provider. Authentication via backend X-Auth-Token only. Competition: WC 2026 (WC, fallback ID 2000).
sync.theSportsDbHint: Free key 123 — enriches missing stadiums and cities for WC matches (league 4429, season 2026). Player images use the same API.
sync.playerImagesHint: Player images are loaded from TheSportsDB, Wikidata and Wikipedia and cached locally. First run runs in background (~20 min) — see sync logs.

email.checklistHint: Without full SMTP configuration, verification, reset and reminder links are only logged in the backend console.
email.reminderLanguageDesc: Tip reminders are sent automatically in each user's language ({langs}) – based on their profile language, same as registration and password reset.
email.reminderLanguageNote: No manual template or AI text is required. Each user receives the appropriate text in their language.

form.morningDigestDesc: Every morning: last night's results, leaderboard, team standing, highlights and today's matches – personalized per user.

backup.title: Data backup
backup.noticeTitle: Back up player and user data
backup.noticeDesc: JSON backup for restoring data. Automatic hourly backups run in the background. Also: Excel export with leaderboard and all tips for offline worst-case evaluation.
backup.autoJobActive: The backend saves player data every hour on the hour ({schedule}, {timezone}). The last {retention} backups are kept.
backup.excelHint: Excel file with leaderboard, all tips, bonus questions, matches and users – for offline worst-case evaluation.
backup.restoreHint: Existing users are updated by email. Missing users are created. Predictions are matched to the corresponding matches.
backup.noBackups: No backups saved on the server yet.
backup.serverUnreachable: Server unreachable. Please check if the backend is running (port 3000).
backup.confirmRestore: Restore data from backup? Existing users will be updated by email.
backup.confirmRestoreServer: Restore data from "{name}"? Existing predictions will be kept.
